content.problemStatement:
Chefina gave Chef a sequence F1,F2,тАж,FNF_1, F_2, \ldots, F_NF1тАЛ,F2тАЛ,тАж,FNтАЛ as a birthday present. She constructed it from a sequence of positive integers A1,A2,тАж,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1тАЛ,A2тАЛ,тАж,ANтАЛ in the following way: for each valid iii, Fi=тИСj=1i(тИТ1)(j+1)тЛЕAjF_i = \sum_{j=1}^i (-1)^{(j+1)} \cdot A_jFiтАЛ=тИСj=1iтАЛ(тИТ1)(j+1)тЛЕAjтАЛ.

Unfortunately, Chef was playing with the sequence FFF and shuffled it. Now, he has a sequence denoted by BBB, but he does not remember FFF. The only things he knows are that FFF is a permutation of BBB and among all such permutations which Chefina could have constructed, her sequence FFF was the lexicographically smallest.

Chef does not want to disappoint Chefina, but he is too busy in the kitchen, so he asked you to help him recover FFF. Find the sequence FFF he got from Chefina or determine that he made another mistake and a sequence that satisfies the given conditions does not exist.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (50 points):** B1,B2,тАж,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1тАЛ,B2тАЛ,тАж,BNтАЛ are pairwise distinct
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,тАж,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1тАЛ,B2тАЛ,тАж,BNтАЛ.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If there is no solution, print a single line containing the string `"NO"` (without quotes).
Otherwise, print two lines.
The first of these lines should contain the string `"YES"` (without quotes).
The second line should contain NNN space-separated integers F1,F2,тАж,FNF_1, F_2, \ldots, F_NF1тАЛ,F2тАЛ,тАж,FNтАЛ.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The only two permutations of BBB are [1,2][1,2][1,2] and [2,1][2,1][2,1]. Out of them, [1,2][1,2][1,2] could not have been generated by Chefina, since it would require A=[1,тИТ1]A = [1,-1]A=[1,тИТ1], which contains a negative element. On the other hand, for [2,1][2,1][2,1], we get A=[2,1]A = [2,1]A=[2,1], so F=[2,1]F = [2,1]F=[2,1] must be the sequence Chefina generated.
